title: Trump Tells The Telegraph NATO Is a 'Paper Tiger,' Remains 'Strongly Considering' US Exit
detail: President Trump told The Telegraph he was still 'strongly considering' pulling the United States out of NATO and called the alliance a 'paper tiger,' citing European allies' refusal to support the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz operations. Trump said the US would no longer guarantee Hormuz security for allies that did not participate in the war effort. Secretary of State Rubio separately reinforced the message on Fox News: 'There's no doubt, unfortunately, after this conflict is concluded, we are going to have to re-examine that relationship... We're going to have to re-examine the value of NATO for our country.' TIME headlined: 'Trump Threatens to Pull U.S. Out of NATO Amid Fallout Over Iran War.' Al Jazeera analyzed how allied governments were pushing back against Trump's Iran war demands. The public statements from both the President and Secretary of State on the same day marked the most concentrated senior US executive-branch challenge to NATO membership in the alliance's history.

title: UK's Starmer Announces Multi-Nation Hormuz Meeting, Defends NATO Against Trump
detail: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that Britain would host a multi-nation meeting of approximately 35 countries focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz — a diplomatic rather than military approach that contrasted with Trump's demand for military coalition contributions. Starmer dismissed Trump's threats as 'noise,' stating: 'Whatever the noise, I'm going to act in the British national interest.' Starmer also publicly defended NATO, calling it 'the single most effective military alliance the world has ever seen,' after Trump branded the alliance a 'paper tiger.' The UK maintained its position of allowing US bombers to use British bases (Lakenheath, Fairford) only for defensive missions against Iranian targets, not offensive Iran operations. US News and Vanguard News both headlined Starmer's defense of the alliance as a direct counterpoint to Trump's 'paper tiger' characterization.
